important_dates.bca/bcb/ews certificate validity: Issued on or after April 1, 2026 and on or before closing date
important_dates.dsc/osc certificate validity: Issued after November 13, 2024 and on or before closing date
important_dates.esm eligibility certificate: Issued/renewed on or after June 25, 2025 and on or before closing date

qualification.details: Eligibility is the most critical aspect of any government job application. HSSC has defined separate eligibility conditions for each post, covering educational qualifications, physical standards, and age limits. | The minimum academic requirement differs based on the post applied for. | For Assistant Superintendent Jail (Cat. No. 1 & 2): | Graduation from any recognized university or its equivalent Hindi or Sanskrit as one of the subjects in Matriculation or higher education | For Prison Warder (Cat. No. 3 & 4): | 10+2 (Senior Secondary) or its equivalent from a recognized education board Hindi or Sanskrit as one of the subjects in Matriculation or higher education | Physical fitness is a key component of the eligibility criteria for both post categories. | Male Candidates (General Category): | Height: 170 cm Chest: 83 cm (unexpanded) / 87 cm (expanded) | Male Candidates (Reserved Categories): | Height: 168 cm Chest: 81 cm (unexpanded) / 85 cm (expanded) | Female Candidates (General Category): | Height: 158 cm | Female Candidates (Reserved Categories): | Height: 156 cm | Candidates who clear the Physical Measurement Test will be required to appear in a Physical Screening Test. This test is qualifying in nature only and does not carry marks. | Age eligibility varies by post group. Reserved category candidates receive relaxation as per Haryana Government norms. | Also read: BEML Operator Recruitment 2026 Notification Out | DON'T MISSRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Posts • Technician • RRB29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Vacancies • Nursing Officer13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Openings • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,318 postsKSP Civil Police Constable Online Form 20263,991 Vacancies • Civil Police Constable • KSP03 Jul 20263,991 postsMPSC 2619 Group C Online Form 20262,619 Openings • Group C17 Jul 20262,619 posts

selection_process: The selection process is multi-staged and merit-based. Here is a complete breakdown of how candidates move from application to final recommendation.

Only candidates who cleared CET (Advt. 01/2025) with minimum qualifying scores are invited to apply. Up to 10 times the total number of advertised posts will be shortlisted for the written/skill examination based on CET merit.
For Police and Prison service posts
candidates are first tested on physical standards (height
chest). Those who qualify proceed to the Physical Endurance Test (PET)
which is qualifying in nature only.
The main OMR-based written examination determines the final merit. Candidates must score a minimum of 50% (General) or 40% (Reserved) in the written exam to be considered for selection.
a merit list is prepared category-wise. If two candidates have equal marks
the older candidate gets precedence. If age is also equal
the candidate with higher CET Phase-I marks is ranked higher.
The appointing department verifies the genuineness of uploaded documents at the time of appointment.

pattern_table.Details: No negative marking for wrong answers; -1 mark for unattempted questions (if fifth circle not filled)

exam_pattern.details: Understanding the exam pattern and syllabus is essential to building an effective preparation strategy. The Phase-II written examination is an offline OMR-based test conducted in Hindi and English (bilingual). | The Group 1 syllabus covers a broader academic scope given the graduation-level qualification requirement. | Section Weightage: | General Awareness, Reasoning, Mathematics, Science, History (including Haryana), Current Affairs, Geography, Civics, Environment, Culture - 20% Computer Fundamentals (MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Internet, Email) - 10% Subject-Related Syllabus - 70% | Subject-Specific Topics (Group 1) include: | General Intelligence & Reasoning General Awareness (India & Haryana focus) Quantitative Aptitude (up to Class 10 level) Hindi & English Language Economics & Governance (Haryana Economy, NABARD, MSME, Insurance) Environmental Studies & Disaster Management | The Group 2 syllabus is aligned with 10+2 level education. | Subject-Specific Topics (Group 2) include: | General Awareness (Indian Constitution basics, Traffic Rules, Welfare Schemes of Haryana) General Intelligence & Reasoning English Language Quantitative Aptitude Hindi Language (Grammar, Composition) Haryana Economy & Rural Marketing Environmental Studies & Disaster Management

how_to_apply: This section explains the complete step-by-step process to submit your online application for HSSC Advt. 06/2026. Read every instruction carefully before filling out the form, as no corrections will be allowed after final submission.
Visit the official recruitment portal: https://adv062026.hryssc.com
Log in using your CET Registration Number from Advt. No. 01/2025
Fill in the Online Detailed Application Form (DAF) carefully
Upload all scanned documents in the prescribed format
Select your post preferences carefully - these cannot be changed later
Submit the form before June 30, 2026 (11:59 PM)
Download and save a printout of the submitted application for future reference
Only candidates who qualified CET (Advt. No. 01/2025) with 50% marks (General) or 40% marks (Reserved categories) are eligible to apply.
Post preferences filled at the time of application are final and cannot be revised under any circumstances.
Documents uploaded at the time of application are the only documents considered at all stages. Documents not uploaded will not be accepted later.
No offline application will be accepted.
The correction/edit window is available from July 1–2, 2026 only.

faq.question: Q1. What is HSSC Recruitment 2026 Advertisement No. 06/2026 about?
faq.answer: HSSC Advt. 06/2026 is a CET Phase-II recruitment drive for Group-C posts in the Haryana Prison Department, including Prison Warder (Male & Female) and Assistant Superintendent Jail (Male & Female). A total of 1,238 vacancies are advertised, and only candidates who qualified CET Advt. No. 01/2025 are eligible to apply.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for HSSC Group-C 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM. A correction window is available from July 1 to July 2, 2026.

faq.question: Q3. Is there any application fee for HSSC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No. There is no application fee required for any candidate, regardless of category.

faq.question: Q4. What is the minimum CET score needed to apply for these posts?
faq.answer: General category candidates must have scored a minimum of 50% in CET Advt. No. 01/2025. Reserved category candidates (both vertical and horizontal) require a minimum of 40%.

faq.question: Q5. What is the exam pattern for HSSC Phase-II 2026?
faq.answer: The exam consists of 100 MCQs for 100 marks, to be completed in 1 hour 45 minutes. It is offline (OMR-based) and bilingual. There is no negative marking for wrong answers, but -1 mark is deducted for questions left unattempted without filling the fifth circle.

faq.question: Q6. Who is eligible for age relaxation in HSSC 2026?
faq.answer: DSC, OSC, BCA, BCB, and EWS candidates receive a 5-year age relaxation. Persons with Disabilities receive up to 10 years of relaxation. Ex-Servicemen receive relaxation equal to their military service plus 3 years.

faq.question: Q7. What happens if two candidates have equal marks in the written exam?
faq.answer: The older candidate gets precedence. If age is also the same, the candidate with higher marks in CET Phase-I will be ranked higher.

faq.question: Q8. Can I change my post preference after submitting the application?
faq.answer: No. Post preferences confirmed at the time of submission are final and irrevocable under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q9. Which certificates are valid for BCA/BCB and EWS categories in this recruitment?
faq.answer: BCA, BCB, and EWS certificates must have been issued on or after April 1, 2026 and on or before the closing date. Certificates issued for Central Government jobs or Panchayati Raj elections are not valid.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification and apply online?
faq.answer: The official advertisement is available at www.hssc.gov.in. Applications must be submitted at https://adv062026.hryssc.com.
